description:
# Why

bl-c4e2 added `.balls/code-refs` to `store_init::ensure_main_gitignore`,
but placed it inside the `if !is_stealth` block alongside `.balls/tasks`
and `.balls/worktree`. That gating is wrong for code-refs.

`.balls/tasks` and `.balls/worktree` are non-stealth-only because they
*do not exist* in stealth mode — stealth keeps task JSON under a
`--tasks-dir` path, with no state worktree and no symlink. So there is
nothing to gitignore.

The code-refs cache is different. `delivery_remote::ensure_cache`
materializes `.balls/code-refs/<hash>.git` relative to the repo root on
any `bl show --resolve-remote`, with no stealth gating whatsoever. A
stealth-mode repo that opts into `--resolve-remote` therefore gets an
untracked `.balls/code-refs/` tree that is NOT gitignored (the stealth
`wanted` list is only `.balls/local`, `.balls-worktrees`). A subsequent
`git add -A` silently commits a forge clone into the project — exactly
the failure mode bl-c4e2 set out to close, still open for stealth repos.

The `review_safety::RUNTIME_PATHS` squash backstop already covers this
mode-independently (it added `.balls/code-refs` unconditionally), so
this is defense-in-depth, not a hole in the last line of defense — but
the gitignore-side defense should not be thinner for stealth than for
non-stealth when the hazard is identical.

# Scope

- `store_init::ensure_main_gitignore`: move `.balls/code-refs` out of
  the `if !is_stealth` block and into the always-`wanted` list, next to
  `.balls/local` (the other path that exists in both modes). Leave
  `.balls/tasks` and `.balls/worktree` non-stealth-gated — that gating
  is correct for them.
- Update the docstring on `ensure_main_gitignore` to reflect that
  `.balls/code-refs` is unconditional.
- A test exercising stealth-mode init (`bl init --tasks-dir ...`) and
  asserting `.gitignore` contains `.balls/code-refs`. `init_stealth.rs`
  is the existing home for stealth init stories.

# Out of scope

`.balls/state-repo` (master_url mode) has a separate, parallel gap —
also missing from both `ensure_main_gitignore` and `RUNTIME_PATHS`.
Tracked/decided separately; do not fold it in here.

# Origin

Follow-up from the bl-c4e2 review pass. bl-c4e2 followed its ball's
explicit scope (\"non-stealth wanted list, mirrors .balls/worktree\"),
which under-covered stealth mode because code-refs, unlike worktree,
is materialized regardless of mode.
title: ensure_main_gitignore: cover .balls/code-refs in stealth mode too
